results.title: Blumenthal Publishes Graham's 'Sledgehammer' Bill Targeting Russia's Energy Projects
results.description: U.S. senators have introduced a revised bill that would significantly expand sanctions against Russia by targeting the country's financial institutions, liquefied natural gas projects, shadow fleet and foreign companies accused of supporting Russia's defense industry. The proposal would also authorize tariffs of up to 100% on major importers of Russian oil and natural gas. Expanded Sanctions Would Target Energy, Banking and Defense The updated legislation, published on the website of Senator Richard Blumenthal, was developed with the participation of the late Senator Lindsey Graham. According to the bill's summary, the measures would impose mandatory sanctions on Russian President Vladimir Putin, senior Russian political and military officials, business leaders, state-owned enterprises and foreign companies that U. S. lawmakers believe support Russia's defense-industrial sector. Also read: Death of a Senator: Lindsey Graham – Media Hysteria & Practical Reality

results.title: National Guard deployment to DC will last through Trump's term
results.description: President Trump's deployment of National Guard troops to the nation's capital is being extended by more than two years and will last until the end of his term.

results.title: Kalonzo dismisses Wantam Movement platform after invitation notice for presidential debate
results.description: Wiper Patriotic Front leader Kalonzo Musyoka has distanced his party, the Azimio la Umoja-One Kenya Coalition, from a newly launched digital platform claiming to give Kenyans a say in selecting the Opposition’s 2027 presidential flagbearer. In a statement issued on Thursday, July 16, 2026, Kalonzo said the platform, unveiled by a lobby group identifying itself [...]

results.title: Dubai Police renew commitment to the UAE leadership on Union Pledge Day
results.description: Dubai: Dubai Police have reaffirmed their commitment to safeguarding the achievements of the United Arab Emirates and advancing its development journey as the nation marks Union Pledge Day, a national occasion commemorating the historic meeting of 18 July 1971 that laid the foundations for the establishment of the UAE. On the occasion, Dubai Police extended their congratulations to President His Highness Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Vice President, Prime Minister and Ruler of Dubai His Highness Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, Vice President, Deputy Prime Minister and Chairman of the Presidential Court His Highness Sheikh Mansour bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Their Highnesses the Members of the Supreme Council and Rulers of the Emirates, Crown Princes, and all citizens and residents across the UAE. Union Pledge Day was designated by the President of the UAE to commemorate the historic gathering held on 18 July 1971, during which the Founding Father, the late Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, together with the Rulers of the Emirates, signed the Union Document and the UAE Constitution and announced the establishment and official name of the United Arab Emirates. Lieutenant General Abdulla Khalifa Al Marri, Commander-in-Chief of Dubai Police, described Union Pledge Day as a deeply rooted national occasion that honours the wisdom and foresight of the Founding Fathers, whose unity, determination and vision laid the foundations of a nation that has become a global model for security, stability, sustainable development and leadership. He said the occasion serves not only as a reminder of a defining chapter in the UAE’s history, but also as an opportunity to renew loyalty to the nation’s leadership, reaffirm commitment to protecting national achievements, strengthen the values of unity, belonging and loyalty, and continue working with dedication and responsibility to reinforce the country’s regional and international standing. Al Marri added that “the late Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan and the Founding Fathers demonstrated exceptional vision and unwavering determination in building a strong federal nation founded on unity, solidarity, justice and the rule of law.” He noted that these enduring values continue to guide the country’s leadership, enabling the UAE to become one of the world’s safest and most prosperous nations. Concluding his remarks, Al Marri said Union Pledge Day is an opportunity to renew Dubai Police’s pledge of loyalty to the nation and its leadership, continue protecting the security of society, preserve the country’s achievements, work in the spirit of one team to enhance quality of life and sustainable development, and ensure that the legacy of the Founding Fathers continues to inspire future generations.

results.title: Strikes strengthen options, U.S. military officials say
results.description: WASHINGTON — Recent waves of U.S. strikes on Iran aimed at forcing open the Strait of Hormuz are also targeting Iranian military capabilities the U.S. would want to destroy before executing more complex operations against Iran, three U.S. officials said.

results.title: Oil prices rise as US-Iran hostilities threaten major shipping routes
results.description: Oil prices rose slightly on Friday following increased military actions between the US and Iran in the Gulf region, with concerns mounting over potential disruptions to key oil transit routes, reported Reuters.
